rulings.Case Title: MAY TSAI et al VS. CONNIE FUNG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT CONNIE FUNG, RONALD FUNG, and, FUNG TRUST DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The facts that gave rise to this complaint arose in 2006 and are barred by the one-year statute of limitations pursuant to CCP section 340 and Menefee vs. Ostawri (1991) 228 Cal.App. 3d 239. Additonal attempts to avoid the statute of limitations by deleting facts that would render the complaint barred and adding conclusory paragraphs that purport to collect damages for harm that occured only within the statutory period renders this complaint additionally barred by the sham pleading doctrine. (See State ex rel. Metz vs. CCC Information Services, Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App. 4th 402.) No. reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petion Compelling Arbitraion And Appointing Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2009, line 6, PETITIONER NAOMI PEARCE Notice Of Hearing On Petion Compelling Arbitraion And Appointing Arbitrator. (Duplicate entry). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continued Hearing On Petition Compelling Arbitration And Appointing Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2009, line 6, PETITIONER NAOMI PEARCE Notice Of Continued Hearing On Petition Compelling Arbitration And Appointing Arbitrator. Petition compelling arbitration is granted. This dispute is subject to a valid arbitration agreement and neither of the exceptions provided in Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2 applies. Defenses; including statute of limitations, will be adjudicated by the arbitrator. Appointment of the arbitrator is governed by Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6. Having received Petitioner's list, the court orders respondents to supply lists of arbitrators no later than close of business July 31, 2009. The court will nominate five persons from the lists submitted. Within five days of receipt of the notice of the nominees from the court, the parties may jointly select an arbitrator. If the parties fail to select an arbitrator within the five day period, the court shall appoint an arbitrator from the nominees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LESLY LOUIE VS. FRANCISCO MOLIERI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT FRANCISCO MOLIERI MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Motion to quash is denied. Five days leave to answer. The complaint and attachments: Notice of Termination of Tenancy, Declaration of Lesly Louie, and Rental Agreement, clearly designates 2260 Judah Street as the "subject property." 37.9B(c) requires only the owner who endeavors to recover possession to supply the listed information, and not all landlords who have an interest in the property. =(302/CWW)

Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT MICHAEL GALLIN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer to complaint is overruled. The court treats the subject demurrer as a motion to strike, granting in part, and denying part. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the three day notice to pay or quit regarding unpaid rent; and three day notices to cure or quit regarding failure to comply with mandatory written procedures of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance to request present sub-tenancies, creation of present illegal subtenancies, and creation of unlawful assignment of tenancy. The remaining notices are stricken. Defendant is granted five days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)
